attacking_style: Rarely initiates attacks himself; relies on opportunistic gains through bonuses, team tempo, and capitalizing on rivals' misfortunes rather than explosive moves. Defensive posture when racing for GC.
team_tactics: DSM (now Bora) uses controlled tempo riding to consolidate positions rather than aggressive domestique launches. Benefits when team can control pace on climbs, struggles when team support collapses (echelons, crashes).
terrain_preference: Moderate climbs where tempo control matters more than explosive accelerations. Exposed in technical descents, high-speed echelon situations, and when requiring sustained isolation on final climbs.
vulnerability: Catastrophic weakness in echelon scenarios (2+ observations of major time losses). Vulnerable to bad luck (crashes) and cannot respond when isolated without team support. Form appears inconsistent year-to-year.
key_patterns: Won Paris-Nice 2021 primarily due to Roglic crashes, not superior climbing ability or attacks
Lost significant time in echelons/wind multiple times as defending champion in 2022
Relies on intermediate bonuses and tactical gains rather than raw climbing power
Defensive racing style: follows moves rather than initiating when under pressure (multiple observations)
Team tempo control critical to success; collapses without domestique support on climbs
Poor descending/technical skills evidenced by crashes and positioning losses
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 showed clear decline from 2021 Paris-Nice win, losing time in echelons as defending champion and crash-affected Tour de Suisse
